originalAlarm.description: Value stored to 'codec_hw_write' during its initialization is never read

model:
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_FSI_AK4642 () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642 () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_SOC_DA7210 () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CONFIG_I2C () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_FSI_DA7210 () Bool
  false)
reasoning: 66517915e09.c L24: codec_hw_write dead store in ak4642_init path. The real condition for the dead store is CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642=T AND CONFIG_I2C=T. The PC is very broad — it includes branches where CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642=F (e.g., FSI_AK4642=T with SOC_AK4642=F is contradictory since FSI_AK4642=T is a derived macro that implies SOC_AK4642=T; also includes standalone SOC_AK4642=F branches). These non-compilable/contradictory configs over-constrain the PC. New bug (same bug as TRANSFORMATION_119), over-constrained PC.
